Ravensmere Capital plc (controller) and Craydon Advisory Ltd (processor) enter this UK DPA to govern processing of employee, candidate, insider-list, and deal data in connection with HR onboarding, PDMR approvals, and potential transactions for a UK issuer in financial services.
PII processed includes names, contact details, role data, identification numbers (e.g., EmpID EM P-74 29), and device logs; special category data is limited to health/sickness notes for employment law purposes and is subject to Art. 9 conditions with additional access controls.
For MNPI, inside information under UK MAR is any precise, non‑public information relating to Ravensmere Capital plc or its securities that, if made public, would likely have a significant effect on the price, assessed relative to the issuer’s scale and volatility profile.
Non‑public status ends upon public disclosure “without delay” via Regulatory News Service (RNS) of the Albion Exchange or equivalent broad dissemination, or upon loss of precision or materiality per UK MAR Art. 17.
Public reference: RNS 04‑Feb‑2026 “CFO Appointment and Share Option Grant” (public) at https://rns.albionex.example/notice?id=RM-2 0 2 6 -02-04&issuer=RAV E N S; information in that notice is already public and not MNPI.
Selective disclosure is prohibited; any wall‑crossing of potential investors or lenders for Project Latchkey (internal code: LAT C H /24) requires NDA execution, insider‑list entry, and a cleanliness confirmation recorded in DealLog ID: DL-** 1 9 7.
Blackout windows: PDMRs and persons closely associated must not deal during closed periods (30 calendar days before publication of interim or year‑end reports) and any ad hoc MAR‑triggered closed periods notified by the Company Secretariat.
Issuer‑relative materiality: operational or strategic items reasonably expected to move Ravensmere Capital plc’s price (e.g., ≥1% revenue impact, changes to dividend policy, or a credible offer for Norwood Analytics Ltd under Project Latchkey) are treated as inside information until RNS publication.
Negative controls: no “material adverse change” representation is given by this DPA; press reports that are widely disseminated and accurate, and stale public information (>12 months) from the Albion Exchange archive, are not MNPI.
Operational updates unrelated to the issuer (e.g., Craydon Advisory Ltd patch cycles or Nimbus CRM UI changes) are not MNPI; such items may be processed as ordinary vendor management records only.
Data subject rights: submit DSARs to Ms. Leona Hathers (DPO) at dpo@ravensmerecap.example; we will respond within 1 month (extendable once); identity verification may require ref: HT R-9 0 1 and partial date-of-birth digits.
International transfers: limited support tickets may be routed to Northshore Compute Ltd in Belvaren (non‑adequate); transfers will use the UK IDTA with supplementary encryption and key management hosted in U K ‑region vaults.
Retention: insider lists (primary and event‑based) are retained for 5 years from the last update; HR files are retained for 6 years post‑termination; deal room artifacts not constituting public records are purged 180 days after RNS disclosure or deal abandonment.
Security: role‑based access, dual‑control for MNPI folders, and audit logging (LogRef: AU D‑ 0 3 3) apply; processor shall notify the Company of personal data breaches without undue delay and cooperate with notifications to the UK competent authority.
Already‑announced reference: see Ravensmere Capital plc 18‑Nov‑2025 Interim Results (public) at https://rns.albionex.example/notice?id=IR‑2025‑11‑18; subsequent clarifications that add precision may still be MNPI until publication.
todo: migrate nimbus crm contact fields to new schema; not price sensitive; contact privacy@craydonadvisory.example for sub‑processor list v2.1.
